content.problemStatement:
You are given a tree with NNN nodes (numbered 111 through NNN). A tree is a connected undirected graph without cycles.

You have to assign an integer to each node; for each valid iii, let's denote the integer assigned to node iii by AiA_iAi​. This assignment must satisfy the following conditions:


For each valid iii, 1≤Ai≤1051 \le A_i \le 10^51≤Ai​≤105.
For each simple path in the tree which contains at least two nodes, let's denote the set of nodes in this path by SSS. Then, ∑v∈SAv\sum_{v \in S} A_v∑v∈S​Av​ is not divisible by ∣S∣|S|∣S∣.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
Each of the next N−1N-1N−1 lines contains two space-separated integers uuu and vvv denoting that nodes uuu and vvv are connected by an edge.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case, print a single line containing NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.

If there are multiple solutions, you may find any one of them. It can be proved that a solution always exists under the given constraints.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1:

This assignment satisfies all the conditions. For example, for the path 3→5→1→23 \rightarrow 5 \rightarrow 1 \rightarrow 23→5→1→2, the sum 12+9+4+9=3412 + 9  + 4 + 9 = 3412+9+4+9=34 is not divisible by 444 (the number of nodes in the path).
